author: johnguilding
content:
Hey, the Private Transfers Engineering team in PSE conducted some user research amongst teams working in the private transfers space a few months back. 

We covered 3 lines of questioning:

1. What technical problems they were facing
2. What core protocol changes they would like to see
3. What non-technical problems they were facing

There was appetite for core protocol changes for privacy amongst these teams. Please take a read of the article, but to summarise here:

* Precompiles for ZK efficiency
* State-tree improvements
* Native shielded pool
* Protocol features that many privacy protocols can share
* Better primitives for storage proofs
* Native account abstraction
* Encrypted data types
* zkWormhole support
* Shorter block times and finality
* Deliberate stances of where not to change the protocol

https://x.com/PrivacyEthereum/status/2054938521366888814
